entries.definition:
w–j–l fear, dread, to be scared, to be apprehensive. Of this
root, three forms occur five times in the Qur’an: وَجِل wajila three
times; وَجِلَة wajilatun once and وَجِلُون wajilūn once.

وَجِل wajila [v. intrans., imperf. يَوْجَل yawjalu] to be seized with
awe (8:2) الَّذِينَ إِذَا ذُكِرَ اللَّهُ وَجِلَتْ قُلُوبُهُمْ those whose hearts tremble
with awe when God is mentioned.

وَجِلَة wajilatun [quasi-fem. act. part., masc. pl. وَجِلُون wajilūn]
trembling, quaking, seized with awe (23:60) وَالَّذِينَ يُؤْتُونَ مَا آتَوْا
وَقُلُوبُهُمْ وَجِلَةٌ أَنَّهُمْ إِلَى رَبِّهِمْ رَاجِعُونَ those who always give, with hearts
that tremble [at the thought] that they must return to Him.
